By a hybrid evaluation framework that combines human-calibrated LLM-as-a-Judge with structural metrics, we analyze model behavior across multiple prompting regimes. Our findings reveal a persistent \emph{decompression gap}: current LLMs often achieve strong surface-level fluency while failing to faithfully instantiate the underlying moral and causal structure encoded in proverbs. We further show that explicit reasoning and iterative refinement can partially mitigate these failures, suggesting that many decompression errors arise from difficulties in translating abstract meaning into narrative form rather than a complete lack of relevant knowledge. Our proposed task naturally extends to other forms of compressed cultural knowledge.

摘要：將一個密集、抽象的諺語轉化為引人入勝且道德上忠實的敘事需要深厚的文化理解和堅實的語義基礎。我們將這個問題框架設為一個\emph{受限語義解壓}任務，並將基於諺語的故事生成作為大型語言模型（LLMs）中抽象到實現的測試平台。專注於波斯語，我們介紹了諺語對齊敘事數據集（PAND），將諺語與人類撰寫的故事和明確的意義配對。通過一個混合評估框架，結合了人類校準的LLM作為評判者和結構性指標，我們分析了模型在多種提示模式下的行為。我們的發現揭示了一個持久的\emph{解壓差距}：當前的LLMs通常在表面流暢性上表現強勁，但未能忠實地體現諺語中編碼的道德和因果結構。我們進一步顯示，明確的推理和迭代的精煉可以部分減輕這些失敗，這表明許多解壓錯誤源於將抽象意義轉化為敘事形式的困難，而不是完全缺乏相關知識。我們提出的任務自然擴展到其他形式的壓縮文化知識。

docs.title: Graph Reduction in Multirelational Networks: A Spreading-Oriented Reduction Benchmark
docs.text:
2606.12581v1 by Mateusz Stolarski, Michał Czuba, Piotr Bielak, Piotr Bródka

Real-world networks are inherently incomplete, noisy, and dynamically evolving, making it difficult to capture all actors and their relationships. Their scale often renders direct analysis computationally demanding. While influence maximisation (IM) has been widely studied, the role of graph reduction as a preprocessing step, and its impact on IM accuracy, remains underexplored. In this work, we introduce the Spreading-Oriented Reduction Benchmark (SORB), an open-source, standardised framework for systematically evaluating IM models across diverse task settings. SORB provides an extensible pipeline operating on a representative collection of real-world networks, including single- and multilayer structures, and accounts for graph reduction directly into the evaluation process. This design shifts the focus from analysing IM algorithms in isolation to quantifying how graph reduction alters predictive performance. Using SORB, we study the effects of sparsification and coarsening across multiple IM scenarios. Our results show that the impact of reduction is strongly dependent on both the network type (single-layer vs. multirelational) and the downstream task ($Gain@k$ vs. $\mathrm{AUC}_{\mathrm{cutoff}}$): sparsification preserves seed set quality on single-layer networks, whereas flattened multilayer networks exhibit systematic ranking degradation regardless of reduction strategy. These findings highlight the importance of reduction-aware, multi-task evaluation when studying spreading processes in complex networks.

摘要：真實世界的網絡本質上是不完整的、嘈雜的，並且是動態演變的，這使得捕捉所有參與者及其關係變得困難。它們的規模往往使得直接分析在計算上要求極高。雖然影響最大化（IM）已被廣泛研究，但圖形簡化作為預處理步驟的角色及其對IM準確性的影響仍然未被深入探討。在本研究中，我們介紹了擴散導向簡化基準（SORB），這是一個開源的、標準化的框架，用於系統性評估不同任務設置下的IM模型。SORB提供了一個可擴展的管道，運行在一組具有代表性的真實世界網絡上，包括單層和多層結構，並將圖形簡化直接納入評估過程中。這一設計將焦點從孤立分析IM算法轉向量化圖形簡化如何改變預測性能。使用SORB，我們研究了在多個IM場景中稀疏化和粗化的影響。我們的結果顯示，簡化的影響在很大程度上取決於網絡類型（單層與多關係）以及下游任務（$Gain@k$ 與 $\mathrm{AUC}_{\mathrm{cutoff}}$）：稀疏化在單層網絡上保持了種子集質量，而扁平的多層網絡則無論簡化策略如何都會顯示出系統性的排名劣化。這些發現突顯了在研究複雜網絡中的擴散過程時，考慮簡化的多任務評估的重要性。

docs.title: EDEN: A Large-Scale Corpus of Clinical Notes for Italian
docs.text:
2606.12569v1 by Tiziano Labruna, Guido Bertolini, Pietro Ferrazzi, Bernardo Magnini

We present EDEN (Emergency Department Electronic Notes), a new and unique large-scale corpus of clinical notes produced in Emergency Departments of Italian hospitals. The corpus, in its current version, is composed of approximately 4 million clinical notes fully anonymized, covering diverse phases of patient care during the stay in the emergency department. In addition, a subset of about six thousand notes has been manually annotated by clinical experts through a structured Case Report Form (CRF) containing 132 items relevant for two patient situations in emergency departments, dyspnea and loss of consciousness. Items may assume numerical values (e.g., for blood saturation), categorical (e.g., for level of consciousness ), binary (e.g., for presence of traumas), and mixed value types. The annotation process involved multiple clinicians and underwent iterative revision to resolve ambiguities in item formulation, resulting in a richly structured (although high imbalanced) resource. The dataset aims to fill a relevant gap of data able to support both the development and the use of Large Language Models in concrete medical applications. We describe the data collection protocol, the on-site anonymisation pipeline, corpus statistics, and the annotation scheme. Finally, we propose CRF-filling as a novel structured information extraction benchmark, and provide zero-shot baseline resulting from Gemma-27B and MedGemma-27B. To the best of our knowledge, the EDEN dataset is the largest freely available corpus of clinical notes existing for the Italian language.

摘要：我們介紹EDEN（急診部電子筆記），這是一個新穎且獨特的大規模臨床筆記語料庫，產自意大利醫院的急診部。該語料庫在當前版本中包含約400萬條完全匿名的臨床筆記，涵蓋患者在急診部住院期間的不同護理階段。 此外，約六千條筆記已由臨床專家通過結構化病例報告表（CRF）手動標註，該表格包含132個與急診部兩種患者情況相關的項目，分別是呼吸困難和意識喪失。 項目可以採用數值（例如，血氧飽和度）、類別（例如，意識水平）、二元（例如，創傷存在與否）和混合值類型。 標註過程涉及多位臨床醫生，並經過多次修訂以解決項目表述中的模糊性，最終形成了一個結構豐富（雖然高度不平衡）的資源。 該數據集旨在填補一個相關的數據空白，以支持大型語言模型在具體醫療應用中的開發和使用。 我們描述了數據收集協議、現場匿名化流程、語料庫統計數據和標註方案。 最後，我們提出CRF填寫作為一個新穎的結構化信息提取基準，並提供來自Gemma-27B和MedGemma-27B的零樣本基線。 據我們所知，EDEN數據集是現存的意大利語臨床筆記中最大的免費可用語料庫。

docs.title: Doc-to-Atom: Learning to Compile and Compose Memory Atoms
docs.text:
2606.12400v1 by Xingjian Diao, Wenbo Li, Yashas Malur Saidutta, Avinash Amballa, Lazar Valkov, Srinivas Chappidi

Long input sequences are central to document understanding and multi-step reasoning in Large Language Models, yet the quadratic cost of attention makes inference both memory-intensive and slow. Context distillation mitigates this by compressing contextual information into model parameters, and recent work such as Doc-to-LoRA amortizes context distillation into a single forward pass that generates one LoRA adapter per document. However, producing a single monolithic adapter for all queries leads to irrelevant-query interference, limited compositional recall, and poor scalability to long-document reasoning. To address these challenges, we propose Doc-to-Atom (Doc2Atom), a compositional parametric memory framework that decomposes each document into semantically typed knowledge atoms. Each atom is compiled into an independent micro-LoRA adapter and a provenance retrieval key. At inference time, a lightweight query router selects and assembles only the relevant atoms into a query-specific adapter, which is then injected into a frozen base model. The entire system is trained end-to-end through a multi-objective distillation framework. Experiments on six diverse QA benchmarks demonstrate that Doc2Atom outperforms Doc-to-LoRA baselines while reducing the memory cost of document internalization.

摘要：長輸入序列對於大型語言模型中的文檔理解和多步推理至關重要，然而注意力的二次成本使得推理既耗費內存又緩慢。上下文蒸餾通過將上下文信息壓縮到模型參數中來減輕這一問題，最近的工作如Doc-to-LoRA將上下文蒸餾攤銷到一個單一的前向傳遞中，為每個文檔生成一個LoRA適配器。然而，為所有查詢生成一個單一的整體適配器會導致無關查詢干擾、有限的組合回憶以及對長文檔推理的擴展性差。為了解決這些挑戰，我們提出了Doc-to-Atom (Doc2Atom)，這是一個組合參數記憶框架，將每個文檔分解為語義類型的知識原子。每個原子被編譯成獨立的微型LoRA適配器和來源檢索鍵。在推理時，一個輕量級的查詢路由器選擇並組裝僅與查詢相關的原子到一個查詢特定的適配器中，然後注入到一個凍結的基礎模型中。整個系統通過多目標蒸餾框架進行端到端訓練。在六個不同的QA基準上的實驗表明，Doc2Atom在減少文檔內部化的內存成本的同時，超越了Doc-to-LoRA基準。

docs.title: Which Models Are Our Models Built On? Auditing Invisible Dependencies in Modern LLMs
docs.text:
2606.12385v1 by Sanjay Adhikesaven, Haoxiang Sun, Sewon Min

Modern LLM training pipelines increasingly rely on other models to generate data, filter corpora, judge outputs, and guide development decisions. These dependencies are recursive: a model may depend on an upstream artifact whose own dependencies are documented only in separate releases and artifacts. As a result, the full dependency structure is fragmented across heterogeneous public artifacts, with complexity and recursive depth far outpacing humans' ability to trace. We introduce ModSleuth, an agentic system that recursively reconstructs LLM dependency graphs from public artifacts with source-grounded evidence. We find that the primary challenge is no longer information extraction, but defining what constitutes a dependency and reconciling artifact references across inconsistent documentation. We address these challenges through a formalization that distinguishes direct and indirect dependencies, represents heterogeneous pipeline roles through operation-centered relationships, and resolves artifact identities across names, versions, and repositories. Applying ModSleuth to four public-artifact-rich LLM releases, we recover 1,060 source-verified dependencies and construct large-scale dependency graphs of modern LLM development. These graphs reveal multi-hop license obligations, train-evaluation coupling, discrepancies between released and training-time artifacts, and documentation inconsistencies that would otherwise be difficult to uncover. We release ModSleuth and the resulting dependency graphs to support transparent analysis of the increasingly complex ecosystems underlying modern LLMs.

摘要：現代的 LLM 訓練管道越來越依賴其他模型來生成數據、篩選語料庫、評估輸出並指導開發決策。這些依賴是遞歸的：一個模型可能依賴於上游的工件，而這些工件的依賴僅在單獨的版本和工件中記錄。因此，完整的依賴結構在異質的公共工件中是碎片化的，複雜性和遞歸深度遠遠超過人類追蹤的能力。我們介紹了 ModSleuth，一個代理系統，能夠從公共工件中遞歸重建 LLM 依賴圖，並提供基於來源的證據。我們發現主要挑戰不再是信息提取，而是定義什麼構成依賴以及在不一致的文檔中調和工件參考。我們通過一種形式化來解決這些挑戰，區分直接和間接依賴，通過以操作為中心的關係來表示異質管道角色，並解決跨名稱、版本和存儲庫的工件身份問題。將 ModSleuth 應用於四個富含公共工件的 LLM 發布，我們恢復了 1,060 個經過來源驗證的依賴，並構建了現代 LLM 開發的大規模依賴圖。這些圖揭示了多跳許可義務、訓練-評估耦合、發布和訓練時工件之間的差異，以及其他難以發現的文檔不一致。我們發布了 ModSleuth 和生成的依賴圖，以支持對現代 LLM 背後日益複雜的生態系統進行透明分析。

docs.title: CCKS: Consensus-based Communication and Knowledge Sharing
docs.text:
2606.12281v1 by Jinyuan Zu, Xiaowei Lv, Yongcai Wang, Deying Li, Yunjun Han, Wenping Chen, Fengyi Zhang, Naiqi Wu

In Decentralized Training and Decentralized Execution (DTDE) for cooperative Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (MARL), action-advising-based knowledge sharing promotes interpretable and scalable cooperation among agents. However, current action advising approaches often adhere too much to the teacher's guidance without evaluating teacher-student compatibility, which causes excessive advising, suboptimal stability, and degraded performance. To overcome these challenges, this paper presents a Consensus-based Communication and Knowledge Sharing (CCKS) framework, which allows agents to adopt recommendations based on consensus-derived constraints and to follow the teacher's instructions more smartly. This mechanism enables agents to balance exploration and learning from experienced teachers, improving overall performance. The key is the consensus model construction, for which we propose to employ contrastive learning to construct consensus models based on local observations in the agents' training phase. In action selection, agents score and choose actions based on consensus and shared knowledge. Designed as a plug-and-play solution, CCKS integrates seamlessly with existing DTDE algorithms. Experiments conducted in the Google Research Football environment and the complex StarCraft II Multi-Agent Challenge demonstrate that the integration with CCKS significantly improves cooperation efficiency, learning speed, and overall performance compared with current DTDE baselines. The code is available at https://github.com/yuanxpy/CCKS.

摘要：在合作式多智能體強化學習（MARL）的去中心化訓練和去中心化執行（DTDE）中，基於行動建議的知識分享促進了智能體之間可解釋且可擴展的合作。然而，目前的行動建議方法往往過於依賴教師的指導，而未評估教師與學生的相容性，這導致了過度建議、次優穩定性和性能下降。為了克服這些挑戰，本文提出了一個基於共識的通信和知識分享（CCKS）框架，該框架允許智能體根據共識導出的約束來採納建議，並更智能地遵循教師的指示。這一機制使智能體能夠在探索與向經驗豐富的教師學習之間取得平衡，從而提高整體性能。關鍵在於共識模型的構建，為此我們提議利用對比學習來根據智能體訓練階段的局部觀察構建共識模型。在行動選擇中，智能體根據共識和共享知識對行動進行評分和選擇。CCKS被設計為即插即用的解決方案，與現有的DTDE算法無縫整合。在Google Research Football環境和複雜的StarCraft II多智能體挑戰中進行的實驗表明，與CCKS的整合顯著提高了合作效率、學習速度和整體性能，與目前的DTDE基準相比更是如此。代碼可在 https://github.com/yuanxpy/CCKS 獲得。

docs.title: The Impossibility of Eliciting Latent Knowledge
docs.text:
2606.12268v1 by Korbinian Friedl, Francis Rhys Ward, Paul Yushin Rapoport, Tom Everitt, Jonathan Richens

Advanced AI systems have extensive knowledge of their environments; in fact, their knowledge may (far) exceed that of their developers or users. Consequently, a desirable property for an AI system is that it is honest -- that it accurately reports its beliefs about the world. Designing an AI system to be honest may be difficult, especially if we want to ask it questions about latent variables in the environment -- variables which are hidden from the human interacting with it. This gives rise to the problem of eliciting latent knowledge (ELK): the problem of training an AI agent to honestly report its beliefs. In this paper, we make ELK formally precise using Causal Influence Diagrams (CIDs). CIDs can be used to describe the relationship between an agent's training environment and its subjective representation of the world. We use CIDs to formalise the distinction between observable and latent variables, to specify what exactly it means for an agent to be honest, and to formally define goal misgeneralisation. We show that, under certain circumstances, developers can incentivise an agent to honestly answer questions by providing correct feedback during training. However, a natural, but undesirable, way for an agent to generalise is to provide answers which humans would evaluate as true, rather than honest answers. We prove an impossibility theorem stating: There is no feedback-based training strategy that depends only on agent behaviour and with certainty produces an honest agent, even if feedback is perfect during training.

摘要：先進的人工智慧系統對其環境擁有廣泛的知識；事實上，它們的知識可能（遠）超過其開發者或使用者的知識。因此，對於一個人工智慧系統來說，一個理想的特性是誠實——即它準確地報告其對世界的信念。設計一個誠實的人工智慧系統可能是困難的，特別是如果我們想要詢問它有關環境中潛在變數的問題——這些變數對與之互動的人類來說是隱藏的。這引發了潛在知識引出問題（ELK）：訓練一個人工智慧代理誠實報告其信念的問題。在本文中，我們使用因果影響圖（CIDs）對ELK進行正式的精確化。CIDs可用於描述代理的訓練環境與其主觀世界表徵之間的關係。我們使用CIDs來形式化可觀察變數和潛在變數之間的區別，明確指定代理誠實的確切含義，並正式定義目標錯誤概括。我們展示，在某些情況下，開發者可以通過在訓練過程中提供正確的反饋來激勵代理誠實回答問題。然而，代理一種自然但不理想的概括方式是提供人類會評價為真實的答案，而不是誠實的答案。我們證明了一個不可能性定理，聲明：沒有依賴於代理行為的基於反饋的訓練策略，能夠確定性地產生一個誠實的代理，即使在訓練過程中反饋是完美的。

docs.title: Adaptive Multi-Resolution Procedural Knowledge Compression for Large Language Models
docs.text:
2606.12203v1 by Changyue Wang, Weihang Su, Qingyao Ai, Yichen Tang, Runzhong Qiao, Xuancheng Li, Min Zhang, Yiqun Liu

Large language models (LLMs) are widely used to tackle complex tasks with autonomous workflows. Recently, reusable natural language skills have emerged as a popular paradigm to inject procedural knowledge into LLM applications. Since popular skills are often invoked repeatedly, placing their full text in every context significantly increases prefill cost and latency. While text compression techniques have the potential to solve this problem, most existing methods are designed to compress factual knowledge in documents instead of procedural knowledge, making them insufficient for skill compression. In this paper, we argue that an effective skill compression method should: 1) preserve logical dependencies among workflows and tool protocols, 2) enable lightweight, offline compression for frequently updated community skills, and 3) be adaptable to varying complexities across skills. To address this, we present SKIM (SKIll coMpression), an adaptive multi-resolution soft token compression framework for procedural skills. Depending on the complexity of each skill, SKIM creates different numbers of soft tokens that not only improve the efficiency of LLM inference, but also preserve the effectiveness of skill usage. Experiments indicate that SKIM compresses skills to 30 to 60 percent of their original token length while preserving task performance better than existing compression methods.We have released our code at https://github.com/bebr2/SKIM .

摘要：大型語言模型（LLMs）被廣泛用於處理具有自主工作流程的複雜任務。最近，可重用的自然語言技能作為一種流行的範式出現，用以將程序知識注入LLM應用中。由於流行的技能經常被重複調用，將其完整文本放入每個上下文中會顯著增加預填成本和延遲。雖然文本壓縮技術有潛力解決這個問題，但大多數現有方法是設計用來壓縮文檔中的事實知識，而不是程序知識，這使得它們對技能壓縮來說不夠充分。在本文中，我們主張一種有效的技能壓縮方法應該：1）保留工作流程和工具協議之間的邏輯依賴，2）能夠為經常更新的社區技能實現輕量級的離線壓縮，並且3）能夠適應不同技能之間的複雜性變化。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了SKIM（SKIll coMpression），一種適應性的多解析度軟令牌壓縮框架，用於程序技能。根據每個技能的複雜性，SKIM創建不同數量的軟令牌，不僅提高了LLM推理的效率，還保留了技能使用的有效性。實驗表明，SKIM將技能壓縮到其原始令牌長度的30%到60%，同時在保持任務性能方面優於現有的壓縮方法。我們已在https://github.com/bebr2/SKIM上發布了我們的代碼。

docs.title: OpenMedReason: Scientific Reasoning Supervision for Medical Vision-Language Models
docs.text:
2606.12169v1 by Negin Baghbanzadeh, Pritam Sarkar, Michael Colacci, Abeer Badawi, Adibvafa Fallahpour, Arash Afkanpour, Leonid Sigal, Ali Etemad, Elham Dolatabadi

High-stakes clinical use of large vision-language models (LVLMs) requires reasoning that is grounded in visual evidence and clinical knowledge, not just correct final answers. We introduce OpenMedReason, a large-scale, open multimodal medical reasoning corpus comprising approximately 450K image-question-answer instances whose reasoning traces are primarily derived from curated biomedical, human-authored scientific articles. OpenMedReason provides high-fidelity supervision beyond synthetic chains of thought, covering diverse medical domain vision modalities such as radiological scans, microscopic images, visible light photographs, charts, and others. We complement it with OpenMedReason-Bench, a held-out benchmark that allows fine-grained evaluation of LVLMs along three complementary axes of capability, including perception, medical knowledge, and rationale, enabling diagnostic evaluation beyond final-answer accuracy. OpenMedReason is a rich training resource that exhibits its effectiveness in both supervised fine-tuning (SFT) and reinforcement-based alignment. Training with OpenMedReason yields a 20% average improvement in VQA accuracy over the base model and achieves performance within 4.2% of the strongest comparable-scale medical LVLMs. Fine-grained performance analysis confirms that the gains are not concentrated in any single axis: OpenMedReason improves perception, medical knowledge, and rationale jointly, and its reasoning traces are preferred over those of the base model in 86.1% of pairwise comparisons. We release the code and dataset at huggingface.co/datasets/neginb/OpenMedReason.

摘要：高風險臨床使用大型視覺-語言模型 (LVLMs) 需要基於視覺證據和臨床知識的推理，而不僅僅是正確的最終答案。我們介紹了 OpenMedReason，一個大規模的開放多模態醫療推理語料庫，包含約 450K 的圖像-問題-答案實例，其推理過程主要來自經過策劃的生物醫學和人類撰寫的科學文章。OpenMedReason 提供了超越合成思維鏈的高保真監督，涵蓋了多樣的醫療領域視覺模態，如放射掃描、顯微圖像、可見光照片、圖表等。我們用 OpenMedReason-Bench 進行補充，這是一個保留的基準，允許在三個互補的能力軸上對 LVLMs 進行細緻的評估，包括感知、醫療知識和推理，使診斷評估超越最終答案的準確性。OpenMedReason 是一個豐富的訓練資源，顯示其在監督細調 (SFT) 和基於增強的對齊中的有效性。使用 OpenMedReason 進行訓練使 VQA 準確性平均提高 20%，並在最強的可比規模醫療 LVLMs 中達到性能在 4.2% 內。細緻的性能分析確認這些增益並不集中於任何單一軸：OpenMedReason 共同改善了感知、醫療知識和推理，其推理過程在 86.1% 的成對比較中優於基準模型。我們在 huggingface.co/datasets/neginb/OpenMedReason 上發布了代碼和數據集。

docs.title: Soft-Prompt Tuning for Fair and Efficient LLM Benchmark Evaluation
docs.text:
2606.12117v1 by Selen Erkan, Bastian Boll, Kristian Kersting, Björn Deiseroth, Letitia Parcalabescu

Benchmark scores often misrepresent a large language model's (LLM's) knowledge, because they rely, e.g., on the model's ability to follow specific formatting requirements. This especially penalizes base models that may know the correct answers but lack the ability -- typically introduced in post-training -- to structure them as instructed. To overcome this, we propose soft-prompt tuning, an efficient, fair, and architecture-agnostic model evaluation. By optimizing only 10 soft-prompt vectors (roughly 0.0006% parameters for a 7B model) over a short tuning period, we adapt models to specific benchmark formats, closing gaps in format-following and ensuring that underlying knowledge is accurately reflected in benchmark scores. This allows one to fairly compare different base models -- trained with various pre-training recipes -- on benchmarks without the need for full post-training. We evaluated soft-prompt tuning across 7 models and 7 datasets. The results show that (a) soft-prompt tuning saturates format-following within 80 steps (~640 samples) making it highly efficient, (b) soft-prompt tuning significantly outperforms zero- and few-shot prompting, surfacing base model knowledge that standard prompting misses, that (c) even post-trained models can benefit from soft-prompts to maximize format compliance, and that (d) soft-prompted base model performance predicts post-trained model rankings more reliably than zero- and few-shot baselines, offering a low-cost proxy for downstream model quality. Our contributions include (1) metrics which disentangle format-following and knowledge accuracy, (2) a fairer benchmarking protocol of LLM knowledge, and (3) a cost- and memory-effective recipe to identify optimal pre-training strategies early in LLM development.

摘要：基準分數常常錯誤地代表大型語言模型（LLM）的知識，因為它們依賴於模型遵循特定格式要求的能力。這特別對那些可能知道正確答案但缺乏在後訓練中通常引入的能力來按照指示結構化答案的基礎模型造成懲罰。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了軟提示調整，這是一種高效、公平且與架構無關的模型評估方法。通過在短期調整期間僅優化10個軟提示向量（對於一個7B模型大約是0.0006%的參數），我們使模型適應特定的基準格式，縮小格式遵循的差距，並確保基礎知識在基準分數中得到準確反映。這使得人們可以公平地比較不同的基礎模型——這些模型是用各種預訓練配方訓練的——在基準上，而無需進行完整的後訓練。我們在7個模型和7個數據集上評估了軟提示調整。結果顯示：（a）軟提示調整在80步內飽和格式遵循（約640個樣本），使其非常高效；（b）軟提示調整顯著優於零次和少次提示，揭示了標準提示所忽略的基礎模型知識；（c）即使是後訓練模型也能從軟提示中受益，以最大化格式遵循；以及（d）軟提示的基礎模型性能比零次和少次基準更可靠地預測後訓練模型的排名，為下游模型質量提供了一個低成本的代理。我們的貢獻包括（1）解開格式遵循和知識準確性的度量；（2）一個更公平的LLM知識基準協議；以及（3）一個成本和內存有效的配方，以便在LLM開發早期識別最佳的預訓練策略。

docs.title: MSUE: Multi-Modal Soccer Understanding Expert
docs.text:
2606.12106v1 by Litao Li, Yibo Yu, Yufeng Hu, Zhuo Yang, Jiali Wen, Yixin Chen, Yixi Zhou

This paper presents our solution to the 2026 SoccerNet VQA Challenge. We first develop a cost-effective data synthesis pipeline driven by a Vision-Language Model (VLM), which systematically restructures raw domain data into diverse VQA samples, including concise answers and long-form responses. Second, we propose MSUE, a multi-expert question answering architecture that employs a Large Language Model (LLM) to dynamically dispatch questions to text, image, and video experts. These experts are instantiated as a strong text baseline Gemini3-Flash, a fine-tuned Qwen3-VL, and an external knowledge base, respectively, working collaboratively to enhance VQA performance. MSUE achieves an accuracy of \textbf{0.95} on the challenge benchmark, securing third place in the leaderboard.

摘要：本論文提出了我們對2026年SoccerNet VQA挑戰賽的解決方案。我們首先開發了一個由視覺-語言模型（VLM）驅動的成本效益數據合成管道，該管道系統性地將原始領域數據重構為多樣的VQA樣本，包括簡潔的答案和長篇回應。其次，我們提出了MSUE，一種多專家問答架構，該架構利用大型語言模型（LLM）動態地將問題分派給文本、圖像和視頻專家。這些專家分別被實例化為強大的文本基線Gemini3-Flash、微調的Qwen3-VL和外部知識庫，共同協作以提升VQA性能。MSUE在挑戰基準上達到了\textbf{0.95}的準確率，在排行榜上獲得第三名。

docs.title: FORT-Searcher: Synthesizing Shortcut-Resistant Search Tasks for Training Deep Search Agents
docs.text:
2606.12087v1 by Jia Deng, Yimeng Chen, Xiaoqing Xiang, Ziyang Zeng, Shuo Tang, Wayne Xin Zhao, Feng Chang, Chuan Hao, Yuan Wei, Ran Tao, Bryan Dai, Ji-Rong Wen

Training deep search agents requires verifiable questions whose answers remain unavailable until sufficient evidence has been acquired through search. Existing synthesis methods often increase apparent difficulty by enriching graph structures, but structural complexity alone does not guarantee realized search difficulty: the intended search process can collapse through a cheaper identifying route. We formalize this gap with a shortcut-aware difficulty framework and identify four actionable shortcut risks: evidence co-coverage, single-clue selectivity, exposed constants, and prior-knowledge binding. To diagnose their realized effects, we use trajectory signatures including solving cost, answer hit time, and prior-shortcut rate. Guided by this framework, we introduce FORT, a Framework of Shortcut-Resistant Training-Data Synthesis. FORT constructs shortcut-resistant training data by controlling shortcut risks across entity selection, evidence graph construction, question formulation, and adversarial refinement. Experiments show that FORT induces longer pre-answer search and fewer shortcut patterns than existing open-source deep search datasets. Using the resulting trajectories, we train FORT-Searcher with supervised fine-tuning (SFT) only, and it achieves the best overall performance among comparable-size open-source search agents on challenging deep search benchmarks. Relevant resources will be made available at https://github.com/RUCAIBox/FORT-Searcher.

摘要：訓練深度搜尋代理需要可驗證的問題，其答案在通過搜尋獲得足夠證據之前無法獲得。現有的綜合方法通常通過豐富圖形結構來增加表面難度，但僅僅依賴結構複雜性並不能保證實際的搜尋難度：預期的搜尋過程可能會通過更便宜的識別路徑崩潰。我們用一個考慮捷徑的難度框架來形式化這一差距，並確定四個可行的捷徑風險：證據共同覆蓋、單一線索選擇性、暴露常數和先驗知識綁定。為了診斷它們的實際影響，我們使用軌跡簽名，包括解決成本、答案命中時間和先前捷徑比率。在這一框架的指導下，我們引入了FORT，一個捷徑抗性訓練數據合成框架。FORT通過控制實體選擇、證據圖構建、問題表述和對抗性精煉中的捷徑風險來構建捷徑抗性的訓練數據。實驗表明，FORT引導的預答案搜尋時間更長，且捷徑模式比現有的開源深度搜尋數據集更少。利用這些結果軌跡，我們僅使用監督微調（SFT）訓練FORT-Searcher，並在具有挑戰性的深度搜尋基準上實現了與相似規模的開源搜尋代理相比最佳的整體性能。相關資源將在 https://github.com/RUCAIBox/FORT-Searcher 上提供。

docs.title: IntElicit: Eliciting and Assessing Contextualized Creativity via Dialogue Policy Optimization
docs.text:
2606.12086v1 by Mingjia Li, Jin Wu, Hong Qian, Wenhao Huang, Yiyang Huang, Yiwen Zhang, Chanjin Zheng, Xiangfeng Wang, Aimin Zhou, Jiajun Guo

Contextualized assessment offers high ecological validity for evaluating creativity but introduces a critical challenge: observed performance may be confounded with cognitive proficiency (domain knowledge) and agency (willingness to engage). Meanwhile, in the age of generative AI, creative problem solving increasingly occurs in tool-mediated and human--AI interactive environments, making fully static assessment less aligned with contemporary creative practice. To address these issues, this paper proposes IntElicit, a framework for eliciting and assessing contextualized creativity via dialogue policy optimization. IntElicit functions as a constrained adaptive AI Interviewer: it provides non-directive knowledge and agency scaffolds in multi-turn interaction to reduce non-creative confounders, while preserving participants' responsibility for generating the creative content being evaluated. Specifically, to tackle sparse rewards and potential reward hacking (e.g., answer dictation) in open-ended educational dialogue, IntElicit introduces a decomposed process reward mechanism. This mechanism aligns the policy with pedagogical elicitation, rewarding prompts that draw out participant reasoning rather than producing optimal answers on their behalf. Extensive experiments, including participant simulation and a human subject study (N=64), show that IntElicit improves elicited creative outcomes over expert-designed baselines. Together, the results suggest that interactive elicitation can reveal creative potential that static FPSP-style assessment may miss, providing a formative and diagnostic lens for contextualized creativity assessment in AI-mediated learning contexts.

摘要：情境評估為評估創造力提供了高生態效度，但也帶來了一個關鍵挑戰：觀察到的表現可能與認知能力（領域知識）和主動性（參與意願）混淆。同時，在生成式人工智慧的時代，創造性問題解決越來越多地發生在工具介導和人類—AI互動的環境中，使得完全靜態的評估與當代創造性實踐的對應性降低。為了解決這些問題，本文提出了IntElicit，一個通過對話政策優化來引出和評估情境創造力的框架。IntElicit作為一個受限的自適應AI面試官運作：它在多輪互動中提供非指導性的知識和主動性支架，以減少非創造性的混淆因素，同時保留參與者對生成被評估創造內容的責任。具體而言，為了應對稀疏的獎勵和潛在的獎勵駭客（例如，答案口述）在開放式教育對話中的問題，IntElicit引入了一個分解的過程獎勵機制。這個機制使政策與教學引導相一致，獎勵引出參與者推理的提示，而不是代表他們產生最佳答案。廣泛的實驗，包括參與者模擬和一項人類受試者研究（N=64），顯示IntElicit在引出創造性成果方面優於專家設計的基準。總體而言，結果表明互動引導可以揭示靜態FPSP風格評估可能忽略的創造潛力，為AI介導學習情境中的情境創造力評估提供了一個形成性和診斷性的視角。

docs.title: Automating Geometry-Intensive Compliance Checking in BIM: Graph-Based Semantic Reasoning Framework
docs.text:
2606.12065v1 by Zixuan Xiao, Pei Troh Koh, Jun Ma, Jack C. P. Cheng

Automating compliance check for geometry-intensive regulations remains a significant technical bottleneck in Building Information Modeling (BIM), primarily due to the semantic disparity between high-level regulatory logic and structured IFC data. Existing methods, often reliant on static rule templates, struggle to traverse multi-hop reasoning chains or resolve latent spatial dependencies across multiple building entities. To address these challenges, a Spatial-Geometric Reasoning System for Building Information Modeling (SGR-BIM) is proposed as an integrative graph-driven reasoning framework. SGR-BIM dynamically constructs a cross-modal knowledge graph that aligns user intent, regulatory semantics, and BIM geometry, enabling interpretable reasoning without rigid hard-coding. Validated on 679 expert-verified queries from fire safety codes, the framework achieves 84.3% accuracy, representing an 8.6% improvement over enhanced-tool single-agent baselines. This research provides a graph-based semantic reasoning paradigm, enhancing the transparency and flexibility of automated geometric compliance check workflows in the Architecture, Engineering, and Construction (AEC) industry.

摘要：自動化幾何密集型法規的合規檢查仍然是建築資訊建模（BIM）中的一個重大技術瓶頸，主要是由於高層次法規邏輯與結構化IFC數據之間的語義差異。現有方法通常依賴靜態規則模板，難以穿越多跳推理鏈或解決多個建築實體之間潛在的空間依賴關係。為了解決這些挑戰，提出了一個建築資訊建模的空間幾何推理系統（SGR-BIM），作為一個綜合的圖驅動推理框架。SGR-BIM動態構建一個跨模態知識圖，對齊用戶意圖、法規語義和BIM幾何，實現可解釋的推理，而無需僵化的硬編碼。在679個經專家驗證的火災安全法規查詢上進行驗證，該框架達到了84.3%的準確率，較增強工具單一代理基準提高了8.6%。這項研究提供了一個基於圖的語義推理範式，增強了建築、工程和施工（AEC）行業自動化幾何合規檢查工作流程的透明度和靈活性。

docs.title: MODF-SIR: A Multi-agent Omni-modal Distilled Framework for Social Intelligence Reasoning
docs.text:
2606.12018v1 by Shang Ma, Jisheng Dang, Wencan Zhang, Yifan Zhang, Bimei Wang, Hong Peng, Bin Hu, Qi Tian, Tat-Seng Chua

We propose a multi-agent collaborative framework built upon a lightweight Multimodal Large Language Model (MLLM), specifically designed for social intelligence reasoning. A key feature of our approach is that both the training and inference phases are augmented via knowledge distillation. Within this architecture, multi-modal data pertinent to social intelligence is precisely localized. Furthermore, relevant long-tail events are identified, extracted, and rendered as formatted, explicit text. This formatting strategy prevents critical long-tail information from being overshadowed by head events and environmental noise during the tokenization process. Specifically, we integrate Test-Time Adaptation (TTA) across the entire reasoning pipeline, encompassing the extraction and representation of long-tail events, Chain-of-Thought (CoT) prompting, and self-reflection. This TTA mechanism is also distillation-enhanced, utilizing Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) to fine-tune the foundation model exclusively for instance-level reasoning. Extensive evaluations against various open-source and proprietary AI models across multiple benchmarks demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed framework. With around 30% of training data from IntentTrain, we achieve state-of-the-art results. Codes are available at https://github.com/eeee-sys/MODF-SIR, demo is available at https://huggingface.co/spaces/Harry-1234/MODF-SIR, LoRA is available at https://huggingface.co/Harry-1234/MODF-SIR and the dataset for training router is available at https://huggingface.co/datasets/Harry-1234/IntentRouterTrain.

摘要：我們提出了一個基於輕量級多模態大型語言模型（MLLM）的多代理協作框架，專門設計用於社會智能推理。我們方法的一個關鍵特徵是訓練和推理階段都通過知識蒸餾進行增強。在這個架構中，與社會智能相關的多模態數據被精確定位。此外，相關的長尾事件被識別、提取並呈現為格式化的明確文本。這種格式化策略防止了關鍵的長尾信息在標記過程中被頭事件和環境噪音所掩蓋。具體而言，我們在整個推理流程中整合了測試時適應（TTA），涵蓋了長尾事件的提取和表示、思維鏈（CoT）提示以及自我反思。這一TTA機制也得到了蒸餾增強，利用低秩適應（LoRA）專門對基礎模型進行微調以實現實例級推理。對各種開源和專有AI模型在多個基準上的廣泛評估顯示了所提框架的有效性。通過約30%的來自IntentTrain的訓練數據，我們實現了最先進的結果。代碼可在 https://github.com/eeee-sys/MODF-SIR 獲得，演示可在 https://huggingface.co/spaces/Harry-1234/MODF-SIR 獲得，LoRA可在 https://huggingface.co/Harry-1234/MODF-SIR 獲得，訓練路由器的數據集可在 https://huggingface.co/datasets/Harry-1234/IntentRouterTrain 獲得。

docs.title: The Art of Interrogation: Consistency Amplifies Factuality in Spatial Reasoning
docs.text:
2606.11918v1 by Theo Uscidda, Marta Tintore Gazulla, Maks Ovsjanikov, Federico Tombari, Leonidas Guibas

Current Large Reasoning Models (LRMs) exhibit remarkable general capabilities but significantly underperform in spatial reasoning tasks. Existing approaches treat this gap as a knowledge deficit, relying on supervised fine-tuning (SFT) to ingest labeled spatial data from external vision sources or synthetic engines. In contrast, we argue that for many tasks, spatial reasoning capabilities are already present in pre-trained LRMs but require alignment through logical coherence under geometric 2D and 3D constraints. In this work, we propose a self-supervised reinforcement learning (RL) framework that targets the internal reasoning process without requiring ground-truth annotations. By formalizing the notion of consistency verifiers -- reward functions that check for geometric and semantic consistency under transformations -- we demonstrate that models can improve their spatial reasoning abilities. We use both image transformations, like flipping, and textual transformations, like swapping the order of objects in the question, and propose a new optimal transport-based RL strategy, OT-GRPO, which is a minimal-matching variant of group relative policy optimization tailored to pairwise verifiers. We show that this label-free consistency training approaches the accuracy of models trained with ground-truth supervision and achieves similar generalization across diverse tasks and data domains.

摘要：目前的大型推理模型（LRMs）展現出卓越的通用能力，但在空間推理任務中表現顯著不足。現有的方法將這一差距視為知識缺失，依賴於監督式微調（SFT）來從外部視覺來源或合成引擎中攝取標記的空間數據。相反，我們主張對於許多任務，空間推理能力已經存在於預訓練的LRMs中，但需要通過幾何2D和3D約束下的邏輯一致性來進行對齊。在這項工作中，我們提出了一個自我監督的強化學習（RL）框架，旨在針對內部推理過程，而不需要真實標註。通過形式化一致性驗證器的概念——檢查在變換下幾何和語義一致性的獎勵函數——我們展示了模型可以提高其空間推理能力。我們使用了圖像變換，如翻轉，以及文本變換，如在問題中交換物體的順序，並提出了一種基於最優運輸的RL策略OT-GRPO，這是一種針對成對驗證器的最小匹配變體的群體相對政策優化。我們顯示這種無標籤的一致性訓練接近於使用真實監督訓練的模型的準確性，並在不同任務和數據領域中達到了類似的泛化能力。

docs.title: GraspLLM: Towards Zero-Shot Generalization on Text-Attributed Graphs with LLMs
docs.text:
2606.11898v2 by Hengyi Feng, Zeang Sheng, Meiyi Qiang, Li Yang, Wentao Zhang

Research on Text-Attributed Graphs (TAGs) has gained significant attention recently due to its broad applications across various real-world data scenarios, such as citation networks, e-commerce platforms, social media, and web pages. Inspired by the remarkable semantic understanding ability of Large Language Models (LLMs), there have been numerous attempts to integrate LLMs into TAGs. However, existing methods still struggle to generalize across diverse graphs and tasks, and their ability to capture transferable graph structural patterns remains limited. To address this, we introduce the GraspLLM, a framework that combines Graph structural comprehension with semantic understanding prowess of LLMs to enhance the cross-dataset and cross-task generalizability. Specifically, we represent node texts from different graphs in a unified semantic space with a frozen general embedding model, on top of which we perform motif-aware contrastive learning across multiple motif-induced adjacency matrices to extract dataset-agnostic structural information. Then, with our proposed optimal contextual subgraph, we extract the most contextually relevant subgraph for each target node and align these subgraphs to the token space of LLM via an alignment projector. Extensive experiments on TAG benchmark datasets spanning diverse domains reveal that GraspLLM consistently outperforms previous LLM-based methods for TAGs, especially in zero-shot scenarios, highlighting its strong generalizability across different datasets and tasks. Our code is available at https://github.com/Heinz217/GraspLLM.

摘要：研究文本屬性圖 (TAGs) 最近因其在各種現實世界數據場景中的廣泛應用而受到極大關注，例如引用網絡、電子商務平台、社交媒體和網頁。受到大型語言模型 (LLMs) 卓越的語義理解能力啟發，已有許多嘗試將 LLMs 整合到 TAGs 中。然而，現有方法在不同圖形和任務之間的泛化能力仍然存在困難，並且它們捕捉可轉移圖形結構模式的能力仍然有限。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了 GraspLLM，一個將圖形結構理解與 LLM 的語義理解能力相結合的框架，以增強跨數據集和跨任務的泛化能力。具體而言，我們使用一個凍結的通用嵌入模型，將來自不同圖形的節點文本表示為統一的語義空間，在此基礎上，我們在多個由主題誘導的鄰接矩陣上進行主題感知對比學習，以提取與數據集無關的結構信息。然後，通過我們提出的最佳上下文子圖，我們為每個目標節點提取最具上下文相關性的子圖，並通過對齊投影器將這些子圖對齊到 LLM 的標記空間。對跨越不同領域的 TAG 基準數據集進行的廣泛實驗顯示，GraspLLM 在 TAGs 的 LLM 基礎方法中始終表現優於之前的方法，特別是在零樣本場景中，突顯了其在不同數據集和任務中的強泛化能力。我們的代碼可在 https://github.com/Heinz217/GraspLLM 獲得。

docs.title: Fine-tuning Multi-modal LLMs with ART: Art-based Reinforcement Training
docs.text:
2606.11854v1 by Michal Chudoba, Sergey Alyaev, Petra Galuscakova, Tomasz Wiktorski

There are two main Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning (PEFT) techniques for Large Language Models (LLMs). While Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) introduces additional weights between the LLM layers, Soft Prompting introduces additional fine-tuning-specific raw tokens to an LLM input. However, both require modification to the computational graphs of precompiled, preoptimized LLMs. As a result, neither is fully supported in high-throughput engines like vLLM. We propose fine-tuning with ART (Art-based Reinforcement Training). The method injects information into a frozen Multimodal Large Language Model (MLLM) by optimizing only its raw visual input, thus enabling the soft-token approach on pre-compiled computational graphs. It relies on backpropagation of gradients back into a plain pixel array and thus supports any fine-tuning objective. Moreover, the optimized visual input can be stylized as task-relevant computational artworks. The approach's effectiveness is confirmed for different sizes of a popular open Qwen architecture and for several textual benchmarks. Specifically, ART reaches accuracy competitive with LoRA across mathematics and structured-tool-use benchmarks.

摘要：有兩種主要的參數高效微調（PEFT）技術用於大型語言模型（LLMs）。低秩適應（LoRA）在LLM層之間引入額外的權重，而軟提示則在LLM輸入中引入額外的微調專用原始標記。然而，兩者都需要對預編譯、預優化的LLM的計算圖進行修改。因此，這兩者在高吞吐量引擎如vLLM中都不完全受支持。我們提出使用ART（基於藝術的增強訓練）進行微調。該方法通過僅優化其原始視覺輸入，將信息注入到一個凍結的多模態大型語言模型（MLLM）中，從而在預編譯的計算圖上啟用軟標記方法。它依賴於將梯度反向傳播回普通像素數組，因此支持任何微調目標。此外，優化的視覺輸入可以被風格化為與任務相關的計算藝術作品。該方法的有效性在不同大小的流行開放Qwen架構和幾個文本基準上得到了證實。具體而言，ART在數學和結構化工具使用基準上達到了與LoRA相競爭的準確性。

docs.title: Task-Aware Structured Memory for Dynamic Multi-modal In-Context Learning
docs.text:
2606.11853v1 by Zhirui Chen, Ziwei Chen, Ling Shao

Multi-modal large language models (MLLMs) depend on in-context learning (ICL) for rapid task adaptation, but their scalability is severely limited by finite context windows and the growing cost of key-value (KV) caches in long multi-modal sequences. Existing memory compression approaches typically rely on rigid token removal or sample-dependent importance estimation, which introduces bias, disrupts semantic structure, particularly for visual representations, and yields static memories that cannot adapt to new queries. We introduce TASM (Task-Aware Structured Memory), a training-free framework that addresses these limitations through task-aware, structure-preserving, and dynamically accessible memory construction. TASM employs task-vector guided compression to replace sample-specific signals with a task-level direction that captures shared relevance across demonstrations. To preserve the underlying manifold, it applies semantics-aware token merging via bipartite graph matching, aggregating tokens without destructive pruning. Finally, TASM structures memory into a hierarchy comprising a compact Core Memory and a Latent Bank, facilitating query-adaptive dynamic retrieval. Evaluations confirm TASM maintains high performance under heavy compression, effectively balancing efficiency with adaptability.

摘要：多模態大型語言模型（MLLMs）依賴於上下文學習（ICL）以快速適應任務，但其可擴展性受到有限上下文窗口和長多模態序列中鍵值（KV）快取日益增長的成本的嚴重限制。現有的記憶壓縮方法通常依賴於僵化的標記移除或樣本依賴的重要性估計，這引入了偏差，破壞了語義結構，特別是對於視覺表示，並產生無法適應新查詢的靜態記憶。我們引入了 TASM（任務感知結構記憶），這是一個無需訓練的框架，通過任務感知、結構保留和動態可訪問的記憶構建來解決這些限制。TASM 採用任務向量引導壓縮，將樣本特定信號替換為捕捉跨示範共享相關性的任務級方向。為了保留底層流形，它通過二分圖匹配應用語義感知的標記合併，聚合標記而不進行破壞性修剪。最後，TASM 將記憶結構化為一個層次，包含一個緊湊的核心記憶和一個潛在庫，促進查詢自適應的動態檢索。評估結果確認 TASM 在重度壓縮下保持高性能，有效平衡了效率與適應性。

docs.title: From Uniform to Learned Graph Priors: Diffusion for Structure Discovery
docs.text:
2606.11831v1 by Qi Shao, Hao Guo, Jiawen Chen, Duxin Chen, Wenwu Yu

Neural relational inference (NRI) methods discover interaction graphs from trajectories through variational reasoning on discrete potential edges. However, these methods typically rely on oversimplified, factorized graph priors. Such priors, typically nearing uniform distributions, treat edges as independent entities. This systemic misalignment does not match the real-world systems and yields diffuse and indecisive edge posteriors limiting the reliability of structural discovery. To address this, we propose \textit{Diff-prior}, a diffusion-parameterized adaptive prior used to calibrate latent graph distribution rather than generate graphs. Our core insight is to reframe prior integration as a learnable denoising-style calibration that organizes scattered, uncertain edge posteriors into a more reliable overall structure which can be trained by the diffusion model. Diff-prior learns an adaptive structure prior that performs structured calibration on the edge posteriors during inference, guiding it towards a distribution closer to the underlying structure. The diff-prior operates before structural sampling and acts as a denoising calibrator directly on the encoder edge distribution, which provides a generic training paradigm over structured variables. Experiments on standard benchmarks validated our framework, and the results indicate that Diff-prior improves the performance of structure inference and generates more decisive edge posteriors across multiple NRI-family architectures. The code is available on https://github.com/Hardy158118/Diffprior.

摘要：神經關係推斷（NRI）方法透過對離散潛在邊的變分推理從軌跡中發現互動圖。然而，這些方法通常依賴於過於簡化的因子化圖先驗。這些先驗通常接近均勻分佈，將邊視為獨立的實體。這種系統性的錯位與現實世界系統不符，產生擴散且不確定的邊後驗，限制了結構發現的可靠性。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了\textit{Diff-prior}，這是一種擴散參數化的自適應先驗，用於校準潛在圖分佈，而不是生成圖。我們的核心見解是將先驗整合重新框架為可學習的去噪風格校準，將分散的不確定邊後驗組織成更可靠的整體結構，這可以通過擴散模型進行訓練。Diff-prior學習一種自適應結構先驗，在推理過程中對邊後驗進行結構化校準，引導其朝向更接近潛在結構的分佈。Diff-prior在結構採樣之前運作，並作為直接對編碼器邊分佈進行去噪校準的工具，這提供了一種針對結構變量的通用訓練範式。在標準基準上的實驗驗證了我們的框架，結果表明Diff-prior提高了結構推斷的性能，並在多個NRI家族架構中生成了更具決斷性的邊後驗。代碼可在https://github.com/Hardy158118/Diffprior上獲得。

docs.title: WorldReasoner: Evaluating Whether Language Model Agents Forecast Events with Valid Reasoning
docs.text:
2606.11816v1 by Yizhou Chi, Eric Chamoun, Zifeng Ding, Andreas Vlachos

Forecasting real-world events requires language-model agents to reason under uncertainty from incomplete, time-bounded information. Yet evaluating whether agents genuinely forecast requires more than final-answer accuracy: a model may be correct by recalling memorized training facts, citing fabricated evidence, or producing an unsupported causal story. We present WorldReasoner, an evaluation framework for temporally valid event forecasting. Each task gives an agent a resolved forecasting question, a simulated forecast date, and access only to evidence available before that date; after resolution, the framework scores the submitted probability, cited evidence, and optional causal event graph. WorldReasoner reports three complementary axes: outcome quality against resolved answers, evidence quality over cited sources, and reasoning quality against post-resolution hindsight graphs. The benchmark is built by an agentic construction pipeline that generates forecasting questions, collects time-stamped evidence, and builds hindsight reference graphs at scale, yielding 345 resolved tasks derived from 14,141 articles with graphs covering 8,087 extracted events. Across six controlled agent settings, temporally valid retrieval is the strongest driver of outcome accuracy; causal graph construction improves key-event recovery; and correct graph-enabled forecasts are more strongly grounded in key events and relevant sources, yet agents still struggle to convert grounded evidence into calibrated probabilities.

摘要：預測真實世界事件需要語言模型代理在不確定性下從不完整的、時間有限的信息中進行推理。然而，評估代理是否真正進行預測需要的不僅僅是最終答案的準確性：一個模型可能通過回憶記憶中的訓練事實、引用虛構的證據或產生不支持的因果故事來正確。我們提出了WorldReasoner，一個用於時間有效事件預測的評估框架。每個任務為代理提供一個解決的預測問題、一個模擬的預測日期，以及僅能訪問在該日期之前可用的證據；在解決後，該框架評分提交的概率、引用的證據和可選的因果事件圖。WorldReasoner報告三個互補的軸心：針對解決答案的結果質量、針對引用來源的證據質量，以及針對解決後的回顧圖的推理質量。該基準是通過一個代理建構管道建立的，該管道生成預測問題、收集時間戳證據，並大規模構建回顧參考圖，產生了345個來自14,141篇文章的解決任務，這些圖涵蓋了8,087個提取的事件。在六個受控的代理設置中，時間有效的檢索是結果準確性的最強驅動因素；因果圖的構建改善了關鍵事件的恢復；並且正確的圖形啟用預測更強烈地基於關鍵事件和相關來源，但代理仍然難以將基於證據的內容轉化為經過校準的概率。

docs.title: Fast Speech Foundation Model Distillation Using Interleaved Stacking
docs.text:
2606.11766v1 by Eungbeom Kim, Kyogu Lee

Distilling a large speech foundation model (SFM) into an efficient student model has been successfully applied to low-resource environments. Although distillation reduces inference latency, it requires an additional student model training. However, the training efficiency of SFM distillation remains underexplored. In this work, we explore training acceleration of SFM distillation to speed up model deployment. We examine the potential of stacking, in which the model depth is progressively increased through training until the target model depth is reached. While existing stacking methods improve training speed, they suffer from performance degradation. To handle this limitation, we propose interleaved stacking, a novel stacking method that consistently preserves layer position throughout the stacking process. This property is particularly critical in SFMs, in which each layer encodes distinct layer-specific knowledge. We validate the effectiveness of the proposed method on SUPERB.

摘要：將大型語音基礎模型（SFM）蒸餾成高效的學生模型已成功應用於資源有限的環境中。儘管蒸餾減少了推理延遲，但它需要額外的學生模型訓練。然而，SFM蒸餾的訓練效率仍然未被充分探索。在這項工作中，我們探索了SFM蒸餾的訓練加速，以加快模型部署。我們檢視了堆疊的潛力，其中模型深度透過訓練逐步增加，直到達到目標模型深度。雖然現有的堆疊方法提高了訓練速度，但它們遭遇了性能下降的問題。為了解決這一限制，我們提出了交錯堆疊，一種新穎的堆疊方法，該方法在整個堆疊過程中始終保持層位置。這一特性在SFM中尤為關鍵，因為每一層編碼了獨特的層特定知識。我們在SUPERB上驗證了所提方法的有效性。

docs.title: From Prompts to Tokens: Internalizing Causal Supervision in Vision-Language Model for Multi-Image Causal Reasoning
docs.text:
2606.11745v1 by Haoping Yu, Yuanxi Li, Jing Ma

Visual causal reasoning is essential for understanding and intervening in the physical world, requiring identification of causal variables from visual inputs and reasoning over intervention effects. Despite recent progress, large vision--language models (VLMs) remain brittle at such tasks, especially for interventional and counterfactual queries over multi-image inputs. Most existing explorations inject causal knowledge via textual prompts, leaving causal mechanisms external to model execution and limiting reliable control during inference. To address this problem, we propose BridgeVLM, which internalizes visual causal reasoning by inducing a causal graph from multi-image inputs and converting it into structured Causal Tokens executed by RAMP layers injected into the LLM decoder for causal message passing. We further introduce a unified training interface M3S for fine-grained causal supervision from different granularities (local/global level). BridgeVLM achieves 54.4% accuracy on intervention tasks on CausalVLBench (vs. 33.2% with prompt-level supervision), improves results on Causal3D from 43.6% to 49.0%, and substantially improves causal structure learning on CausalVLBench ($F_1$: 33.4% $\rightarrow$ 75.1%).

摘要：視覺因果推理對於理解和介入物理世界至關重要，這需要從視覺輸入中識別因果變量並推理介入效果。儘管最近取得了一些進展，但大型視覺-語言模型（VLMs）在這類任務上仍然脆弱，特別是在多圖像輸入的介入和反事實查詢上。現有的大多數探索通過文本提示注入因果知識，將因果機制置於模型執行之外，並限制了推理過程中的可靠控制。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了BridgeVLM，它通過從多圖像輸入中誘導因果圖並將其轉換為結構化的因果標記，內化視覺因果推理，這些因果標記由RAMP層注入到LLM解碼器中以進行因果信息傳遞。我們進一步引入了一個統一的訓練介面M3S，以便從不同的粒度（局部/全局層級）進行細緻的因果監督。BridgeVLM在CausalVLBench的介入任務上達到了54.4%的準確率（相比之下，使用提示級別監督為33.2%），在Causal3D上的結果從43.6%提升至49.0%，並在CausalVLBench上顯著改善了因果結構學習（$F_1$: 33.4% $\rightarrow$ 75.1%）。

docs.title: Hey Chat, Can You Teach Me? Structuring Socratic Dialogue for Human Learning in the Wild
docs.text:
2606.11744v1 by Sidney Tio, Arunesh Sinha, Pradeep Varakantham

Large language models are now widely used for everyday learning, but the underlying interactions are typically unstructured chats rather than following a curriculum. Unlike formal online learning systems, these interactions carry no prior record of the student, so any estimate of what the student already knows must be inferred from the dialogue itself. We show that this gap is not closed by scaling models alone. Frontier and education-tuned LLMs perform poorly when asked to tutor a student over an extended session, because doing so requires three things at once. The tutor must sequence a curriculum, conduct Socratic dialogue, and infer the student's knowledge state from that dialogue. We propose separating these responsibilities. Given a student query, our system constructs a prerequisite knowledge graph in which subtopics are nodes and dependencies are edges, and frames tutoring as deciding which node to teach next and how many dialogue turns to spend on it before moving on. A lightweight PPO policy handles this sequencing decision, while an LLM conducts the Socratic exchange at the chosen node and returns a signal of student progress. Across held-out STEM and non-STEM topics, our PPO-paired tutor outperforms heuristic baselines, frontier general-purpose models, and a model specialised for Socratic dialogue: on both the rate at which students reach full curriculum mastery and the number of turns required. Explicit curriculum structure delivers gains that scaling the underlying model does not.

摘要：大型語言模型現在被廣泛用於日常學習，但其背後的互動通常是非結構化的對話，而不是遵循課程。與正式的在線學習系統不同，這些互動沒有學生的先前記錄，因此對學生已經知道的內容的任何估計都必須從對話本身推斷。我們顯示僅僅擴大模型並不能填補這一空白。當被要求在長時間的會話中輔導學生時，前沿和教育調整的LLM表現不佳，因為這樣做同時需要三件事情。輔導者必須安排課程，進行蘇格拉底式對話，並從該對話中推斷學生的知識狀態。我們建議將這些責任分開。根據學生的查詢，我們的系統構建一個先決知識圖，其中子主題是節點，依賴關係是邊，並將輔導框架化為決定下一個教學的節點以及在轉移之前要花多少對話輪次。輕量級的PPO策略處理這一排序決策，而LLM則在選定的節點進行蘇格拉底式交流並返回學生進展的信號。在保留的STEM和非STEM主題中，我們的PPO配對輔導者在學生達到完整課程掌握的速度和所需的輪次上表現優於啟發式基準、前沿通用模型以及專門針對蘇格拉底對話的模型：明確的課程結構帶來的收益是擴大基礎模型所無法實現的。

docs.title: T2S: A Rehearsal-Based Approach for Extraction-Resistant Model Watermarking
docs.text:
2606.11698v1 by Jian-Ping Mei, Weibin Zhang, Ao Yao, Tiantian Zhu, Jie Xiao

Model watermarking safeguards AI model intellectual property by embedding distinctive knowledge that induces unique behavioral signatures. The primary technical challenge lies in ensuring watermark robustness against various post-processing attacks on the watermarked model. Model extraction attacks emerge as the most severe threat, where adversaries exploit prediction outputs to train surrogate models that illegally replicate the original model's functionality. In this work, we propose a rehearsal-based watermark embedding framework to enhance the robustness of model watermarks against model extraction attacks. By simulating the extraction process, our method leverages the loss of a \textit{simulated stolen model} on a trigger set as a training signal to fine-tune the watermark knowledge within the target model. This fine-tuning step encourages the watermark to be embedded in a way that boosts transferability, thereby increasing its chances of persisting and remaining detectable in stolen models. Comprehensive experiments conducted under diverse settings demonstrate that the proposed method significantly improves the robustness of model watermarks against both model extraction and subsequent watermark removal attacks.

摘要：模型水印透過嵌入獨特的知識來保護 AI 模型的智慧財產，這些知識會引發獨特的行為特徵。主要的技術挑戰在於確保水印對於各種後處理攻擊的穩健性。模型提取攻擊被視為最嚴重的威脅，對手利用預測輸出來訓練替代模型，非法複製原始模型的功能。在本研究中，我們提出了一種基於重複訓練的水印嵌入框架，以增強模型水印對抗模型提取攻擊的穩健性。通過模擬提取過程，我們的方法利用在觸發集上“模擬被盜模型”的損失作為訓練信號，以微調目標模型中的水印知識。這一微調步驟促使水印以提高可轉移性的方式嵌入，從而增加其在被盜模型中持續存在和可檢測的機會。在多種環境下進行的全面實驗顯示，所提出的方法顯著提高了模型水印對抗模型提取和隨後水印移除攻擊的穩健性。

docs.title: Can AI Reason Like an Urban Planner? Benchmarking Large Language Models Against Professional Judgment
docs.text:
2606.11678v1 by Yijie Deng, He Zhu, Wen Wang, Junyou Su, Minxin Chen, Wenjia Zhang

Problem, Research Strategy, and Findings: The rise of large language models (LLMs) raises a key question for urban planning: which forms of professional planning knowledge can AI replicate, and which still require human judgment? Although AI tools are increasingly used in planning practice, there is still no systematic framework for testing whether they can reason with the contextual sensitivity, value awareness, and institutional literacy central to planning expertise. This paper introduces Urban Planning Bench (UPBench), a domain-specific evaluation framework that assesses LLM reasoning through a 4x5 matrix of four knowledge pillars and five cognitive levels adapted from Bloom's revised taxonomy. Evaluating 25 LLMs with automated scoring and expert review, we find a non-monotonic cognitive curve: models perform better on higher-order analytical tasks than on factual recall and integrative judgment. This suggests that planning knowledge often treated as lower-order is deeply shaped by institutional, jurisdictional, and temporal context, making it hard for LLMs to generalize. We summarize these limits as four epistemic diagnostics: regulatory hallucination, conceptual conflation, wickedness paralysis, and phronetic deficit. Takeaway for Practice: The findings support differential delegation in planning. LLMs can assist with cross-disciplinary synthesis, literature review, scenario generation, and preliminary policy analysis. However, they remain unreliable for jurisdiction-specific regulation, normative conflict resolution, and context-sensitive procedure. Agencies should require verification for AI-assisted regulatory analysis, while planning education should emphasize institutional literacy, normative judgment, and contextual sensitivity.

摘要：問題、研究策略與發現：大型語言模型（LLMs）的興起對城市規劃提出了一個關鍵問題：哪些形式的專業規劃知識可以被人工智慧複製，而哪些仍然需要人類的判斷？儘管人工智慧工具在規劃實踐中越來越常被使用，但目前仍然沒有系統性的框架來測試它們是否能夠具備對規劃專業知識至關重要的情境敏感性、價值意識和制度素養。本文介紹了城市規劃基準（UPBench），這是一個特定領域的評估框架，通過一個4x5的矩陣來評估LLM的推理，該矩陣由四個知識支柱和五個認知層次組成，這些層次是根據布魯姆修訂的分類法進行調整的。通過自動評分和專家審查對25個LLM進行評估，我們發現了一個非單調的認知曲線：模型在高階分析任務上的表現優於事實回憶和綜合判斷。這表明，通常被視為低階的規劃知識深受制度、管轄區和時間背景的影響，使得LLM難以進行概括。我們將這些限制總結為四個認識診斷：監管幻覺、概念混淆、惡性癱瘓和實踐缺失。實踐啟示：這些發現支持規劃中的差異化委派。LLMs可以協助跨學科的綜合、文獻回顧、情境生成和初步政策分析。然而，它們在特定管轄區的監管、規範衝突解決和情境敏感程序方面仍然不可靠。機構應要求對AI輔助的監管分析進行驗證，而規劃教育應強調制度素養、規範判斷和情境敏感性。

docs.title: Lung-R1: A Knowledge Graph-Guided LLM for Pulmonary Diagnostic Reasoning
docs.text:
2606.11675v1 by Haoyang Zeng, Yuanxi Fu, Rongzhen Li, Yuming Yang, Xiao Sun, Jingwang Huang, Gujie Shao, Guohui Xiang, Quan Lu, Dongfan Ye, Xuetao Chen, Jiang Zhong, Kaiwen Wei, Zhi Xu

Diagnosing pulmonary diseases requires integrating heterogeneous evidence amid phenotypic variability and cross-disease overlap. Although large language models (LLMs) have shown progress on pulmonary knowledge question answering (QA) and information-processing tasks, reliable pulmonary diagnosis requires patient-specific, relation-aware reasoning over electronic medical record (EMR) evidence rather than isolated knowledge recall. We define this gap between pulmonary knowledge and case-level diagnostic reasoning as the Pulmonary Knowledge-to-Diagnosis Gap. To address it, we introduce LungKG, the first structured pulmonary knowledge graph for diagnostic knowledge organization and record-grounded reasoning. LungKG contains 59,038 nodes and 164,308 edges across 15 entity types and 112 relation types, serving as both a reusable pulmonary knowledge resource and the foundation for LungKG-guided model adaptation. Built on LungKG, we propose Lung-R1, a LungKG-guided pulmonary LLM trained through KG-constrained reasoning-chain construction and KG-guided reinforcement learning. In a 20-system evaluation, Lung-R1-14B achieves state-of-the-art performance across Choice, Pulmonary-QA, and EMR Diagnosis, reaching an EMR Diagnosis score of 4.3583 and surpassing the strongest non-Lung-R1 baseline by 0.1476 points. These results demonstrate the value of LungKG-guided training for EMR-based pulmonary diagnosis.

摘要：診斷肺部疾病需要整合異質證據，考慮表型變異性和跨疾病重疊。儘管大型語言模型（LLMs）在肺部知識問題回答（QA）和信息處理任務上已顯示出進展，但可靠的肺部診斷需要針對患者的特定情況進行關係感知推理，基於電子病歷（EMR）證據，而不是孤立的知識回憶。我們將肺部知識與案例級診斷推理之間的差距定義為肺部知識到診斷的差距。為了解決這一問題，我們介紹了LungKG，這是第一個結構化的肺部知識圖，用於診斷知識組織和基於記錄的推理。LungKG包含59,038個節點和164,308條邊，涵蓋15種實體類型和112種關係類型，既作為可重用的肺部知識資源，也為LungKG引導的模型適應奠定基礎。基於LungKG，我們提出了Lung-R1，一個通過KG約束推理鏈構建和KG引導強化學習訓練的LungKG引導肺部LLM。在20個系統的評估中，Lung-R1-14B在Choice、Pulmonary-QA和EMR Diagnosis上達到了最先進的性能，EMR Diagnosis得分為4.3583，超過了最強的非Lung-R1基準0.1476分。這些結果展示了LungKG引導訓練在基於EMR的肺部診斷中的價值。

docs.title: TAROT: Task-Adaptive Refinement of LLM-prior Graphs for Few-shot Tabular Learning
docs.text:
2606.11640v1 by Ruxue Shi, Yili Wang, Mengnan Du, Hangting Ye, Yi Chang, Xin Wang

Few-shot tabular learning provides a cost-effective approach for real-world applications where annotation is costly and collecting sufficient samples for new tasks is difficult. Existing Traditional and LLM-based methods have demonstrated effectiveness in few-shot scenarios. However, traditional methods need additional training on unlabeled or generated data, which incur significant computational overhead. In addition, LLM-based methods that directly feed raw tabular data into LLMs raise privacy and compliance concerns. More importantly, both paradigms largely overlook the semantic relationships between features, which provide structural and semantic prior for constructing a semantic graph. Semantic graph is essential for modeling meaningful feature interactions in few-shot scenarios. In this paper, we propose TAROT, a GNN-based framework that encodes the structural and semantic prior by constructing and refining a task-adaptive semantic graph from this prior, thereby improving predictive performance in few-shot tabular learning. TAROT first encodes heterogeneous tabular data into unified node semantic representations via a Unified Semantic Tabular Node Encoder (USTNE). Then, it prompts LLMs to infer the semantic relationship between features based on the task description and feature names to construct a semantic graph. To mitigate structural noise introduced by the hallucination of LLMs, TAROT introduces Task-adaptive Semantic Graph Refinement that prunes spurious or task-unrelated edges and adds missing task-related ones, aligning the graph structure with the downstream objective. Finally, a GNN performs message passing over the refined graph to capture task-related semantic dependencies for prediction. Extensive experiments on various few-shot tabular learning benchmarks demonstrate the superior performance of TAROT, establishing it as a state-of-the-art approach in this domain.

摘要：少樣本表格學習為現實世界應用提供了一種具成本效益的方法，因為標註成本高且為新任務收集足夠樣本困難。現有的傳統方法和基於大型語言模型（LLM）的方法在少樣本場景中已顯示出有效性。然而，傳統方法需要在未標註或生成數據上進行額外訓練，這會產生顯著的計算開銷。此外，將原始表格數據直接輸入LLM的基於LLM的方法引發了隱私和合規性問題。更重要的是，這兩種範式在很大程度上忽視了特徵之間的語義關係，而這些關係為構建語義圖提供了結構性和語義性先驗。語義圖對於在少樣本場景中建模有意義的特徵交互至關重要。在本文中，我們提出了TAROT，一種基於圖神經網絡（GNN）的框架，通過從這些先驗中構建和完善一個任務自適應的語義圖來編碼結構性和語義性先驗，從而提高少樣本表格學習中的預測性能。TAROT首先通過統一語義表格節點編碼器（USTNE）將異質表格數據編碼為統一的節點語義表示。然後，它促使LLM根據任務描述和特徵名稱推斷特徵之間的語義關係，以構建語義圖。為了減輕LLM的幻覺所引入的結構噪音，TAROT引入了任務自適應語義圖精煉，該過程修剪虛假或與任務無關的邊，並添加缺失的與任務相關的邊，將圖結構與下游目標對齊。最後，GNN在精煉後的圖上執行消息傳遞，以捕捉與任務相關的語義依賴性以進行預測。在各種少樣本表格學習基準上的廣泛實驗證明了TAROT的卓越性能，確立了它在該領域的最先進方法。

docs.title: Physics-Distilled Neural Network enabled by Large Language Models for Manufacturing Process-Property Predictive Modeling
docs.text:
2606.11605v1 by Ge Song, Kiarash Naghavi Khanghah, Anandkumar Patel, Rajiv Malhotra, Hongyi Xu

Predicting process-property relationships in manufacturing is often challenged by high experimental costs and the limited interpretability of complex 'black-box' models. This paper proposes a novel knowledge distillation framework designed to achieve high-accuracy predictions in data-scarce scenarios. The framework integrates analytical physics priors, which are systematically extracted from scientific literature via Large Language Models, into a privileged teacher model. We employ a Graph-Masked Attention layer to capture the complex physical dependencies among input variables showing strict setpoints or a combination of static and high-frequency temporal signatures. This privileged knowledge is distilled into a lightweight student predictor for inference. The feasibility and robustness of the framework are evaluated through a comprehensive experiment across five diverse manufacturing processes. To ensure statistical reliability, given the small dataset sizes, a repeated K-fold cross-validation technique is employed to quantify model stability and generalization. Results indicate that the proposed framework consistently achieves high predictive accuracy across all evaluated domains. Most importantly, the architecture demonstrates significant fault tolerance by maintaining robust predictive performance even in scenarios where LLM-derived analytical priors are suboptimal or incomplete. Furthermore, the student predictor achieves an inference frequency exceeding 6000 Hz, which facilitates real-time edge deployment on standard industrial hardware. This work provides a scalable solution for bridging the gap between theoretical physics and real-time industrial monitoring in data-limited environments.

摘要：預測製造中的過程-性質關係常常面臨高實驗成本和複雜的「黑箱」模型的有限可解釋性挑戰。本文提出了一種新穎的知識蒸餾框架，旨在實現數據稀缺場景中的高準確性預測。該框架將從科學文獻中系統提取的分析物理先驗，通過大型語言模型整合到一個特權教師模型中。我們採用圖掩蔽注意力層來捕捉輸入變量之間的複雜物理依賴性，這些變量顯示出嚴格的設定點或靜態與高頻時間特徵的組合。這些特權知識被蒸餾成一個輕量級的學生預測器以進行推斷。通過在五種不同的製造過程中進行全面實驗來評估該框架的可行性和穩健性。為了確保統計可靠性，考慮到小數據集的大小，採用了重複的K折交叉驗證技術來量化模型的穩定性和泛化能力。結果表明，所提出的框架在所有評估領域中始終實現高預測準確性。最重要的是，該架構顯示出顯著的容錯能力，即使在LLM衍生的分析先驗不理想或不完整的情況下，仍能保持穩健的預測性能。此外，學生預測器的推斷頻率超過6000 Hz，這使得在標準工業硬件上實現實時邊緣部署成為可能。這項工作為在數據有限的環境中彌合理論物理與實時工業監控之間的鴻溝提供了一個可擴展的解決方案。

docs.title: Model-Based and Data-Driven Hierarchical Control and Topology Co-Design for Robust Networked Systems
docs.text:
2606.11596v1 by Shirantha Welikala, Zihao Song, Hai Lin, Panos J. Antsaklis

In this paper, we consider a class of networked systems comprising an interconnected set of linear subsystems, disturbance inputs, and performance outputs. Using dissipativity theory, we first propose a model-based hierarchical control design strategy to ensure the closed-loop networked system is dissipative from its disturbance inputs to performance outputs. This involves designing local controllers for each subsystem to enforce local dissipativity guarantees, which are then exploited to co-design distributed global controllers and the interconnection topology to enforce global dissipativity guarantees while optimizing interconnection topology costs. The overall design process requires only solving a sequence of linear matrix inequality (LMI) problems, thereby retaining compositionality and decentralizability while avoiding non-convex, iterative design processes that are inefficient and centralized. This model-based hierarchical control design strategy assumes the knowledge of the subsystem dynamics, which may not hold in many real-world networked systems. Motivated by this, we also propose a data-driven hierarchical control design strategy that assumes only the availability of rich input-state-output trajectory data from the subsystems. The proposed data-driven design process assumes that the unknown disturbances affecting the subsystem dynamics are bounded by a quadratic matrix inequality (relaxing conventional bounds) and accounts for this by using the matrix S-lemma. Finally, the effectiveness of the proposed model-based and data-driven hierarchical control designs is illustrated for a networked system representing a DC microgrid, with the aim of enforcing robust (dissipative) voltage regulation and current sharing.

摘要：在本文中，我們考慮一類由互聯的線性子系統、擾動輸入和性能輸出組成的網絡系統。利用耗散理論，我們首先提出一種基於模型的分層控制設計策略，以確保閉環網絡系統從其擾動輸入到性能輸出是耗散的。這涉及為每個子系統設計局部控制器，以強制執行局部耗散性保證，然後利用這些保證共同設計分佈式全局控制器和互聯拓撲，以強制執行全局耗散性保證，同時優化互聯拓撲成本。整體設計過程僅需解決一系列線性矩陣不等式（LMI）問題，從而保持組合性和去中心化，同時避免效率低下且集中化的非凸迭代設計過程。這種基於模型的分層控制設計策略假設已知子系統動態，但在許多現實世界的網絡系統中可能並不成立。因此，我們還提出了一種數據驅動的分層控制設計策略，僅假設可獲得來自子系統的豐富輸入-狀態-輸出軌跡數據。所提出的數據驅動設計過程假設影響子系統動態的未知擾動受到二次矩陣不等式的界限（放寬傳統界限），並通過使用矩陣S-引理來考慮這一點。最後，所提出的基於模型和數據驅動的分層控制設計在代表直流微電網的網絡系統中得到了有效性展示，旨在強制執行穩健（耗散）電壓調節和電流共享。

docs.title: GraphInfer-Bench: Benchmarking LLM's Inference Capability on Graphs
docs.text:
2606.11562v1 by Zhuoyi Peng, Jingzhou Jiang, Hanlin Gu, Lixin Fan, Yi Yang

Graph analysis underlies many applications whose answers cannot be looked up in a single record or retrieved along a path: laundering rings, drug repurposing, user preference, and scientific theme are all inferred from a node together with its neighbourhood. We introduce GraphInfer-Bench, a benchmark for whether LLMs can perform this graph inference: producing an open-ended answer that no single node supports and no path retrieves. Existing graph-QA protocols cannot test this capability: algorithm simulation, node classification, single-node description, KG-QA, and GraphRAG all admit answers retrievable from one node or along a path. GraphInfer-Bench defines five tasks along Description (what a region is) and Comparison (how regions differ), each constructed so the ground truth lives in no single node. The release contains 42,000 samples across six real-world graphs, produced automatically and screened by a four-layer quality-control protocol. We evaluate four method families against the same tasks: graph-token alignment models, zero-shot frontier closed-source LLMs, Graph2Text supervised fine-tuning, and plain GNNs as a structural reference. No method family closes the gap. Graph-token alignment partially handles description tasks (relational, theme) but collapses on comparison tasks. Frontier LLMs lead on outlier detection and community partition among LLM-based methods but lag on masked-node prediction. Graph2Text SFT is the strongest LLM-based method on the description side yet falls behind frontier LLMs on comparison. Across every task, plain GNNs match or beat the strongest LLM-based row, with the largest margin on community detection. GraphInfer-Bench surfaces graph inference as an open capability gap rather than a property of any one architecture.

摘要：圖形分析是許多應用的基礎，這些應用的答案無法在單一記錄中查找或沿著某條路徑檢索：洗錢環、藥物再利用、用戶偏好和科學主題都是從一個節點及其鄰域推斷而來的。我們推出了GraphInfer-Bench，這是一個基準，用於檢測LLMs是否能夠執行這種圖形推斷：產生一個沒有單一節點支持且無法沿著任何路徑檢索的開放式答案。現有的圖形問答協議無法測試這一能力：算法模擬、節點分類、單節點描述、KG-QA和GraphRAG都允許從一個節點或沿著一條路徑檢索答案。GraphInfer-Bench定義了五個任務，涵蓋描述（某個區域是什麼）和比較（區域之間的差異），每個任務的設計使得真實答案不會存在於任何單一節點中。該釋放包含42,000個樣本，來自六個真實世界的圖形，自動生成並經過四層質量控制協議篩選。我們針對相同任務評估了四個方法系列：圖形標記對齊模型、零樣本前沿封閉源LLMs、Graph2Text監督微調，以及作為結構參考的普通GNN。沒有任何方法系列縮小了差距。圖形標記對齊在描述任務（關係、主題）上部分處理，但在比較任務上失敗。前沿LLMs在異常檢測和社區劃分方面領先於基於LLM的方法，但在遮蔽節點預測上落後。Graph2Text SFT在描述方面是最強的基於LLM的方法，但在比較上落後於前沿LLMs。在每個任務中，普通GNN的表現與最強的基於LLM的方法相當或更好，在社區檢測上差距最大。GraphInfer-Bench將圖形推斷呈現為一個開放的能力差距，而不是任何一種架構的特性。

docs.title: LLMs+Graphs: Toward Graph-Native, Synergistic AI Systems
docs.text:
2606.11560v1 by Arijit Khan, Longxu Sun, Xin Huang

Large Language Models (LLMs) have advanced rapidly, but their limitations in structured and multi-hop reasoning underscore the need for graph-native, synergistic artificial intelligence (AI) systems. Graph-structured data underpins critical applications across social, biological, financial, transportation, web, and knowledge domains, making it essential to understand how LLMs can leverage graph computation for grounded, context-rich inference. Three complementary synergies are emerging: LLMs augmented with graph computation for retrieval and reasoning; bidirectional integration between LLMs and knowledge graphs (KGs), where LLMs support KG construction and curation while KGs enforce semantic constraints and factual consistency; and AI agents strengthened by graph algorithms for planning, decision making, and multi-step reasoning. In parallel, LLMs introduce new capabilities for graph data management and graph machine learning (ML) through natural language interfaces and hybrid LLM-graph neural network (GNN) pipelines. This tutorial synthesizes the algorithms, systems, and design principles driving these converging directions, offering data science and data mining researchers a unified perspective on integrating LLMs, graph data management, graph mining, graph ML, and agentic computation into next-generation graph-native AI systems.

摘要：大型語言模型（LLMs）迅速發展，但它們在結構化和多跳推理方面的局限性凸顯了對圖形原生、協同人工智慧（AI）系統的需求。圖形結構數據支撐著社會、生物、金融、交通、網絡和知識領域的關鍵應用，因此理解LLMs如何利用圖形計算進行基於實體的、豐富上下文的推理至關重要。三種互補的協同效應正在出現：增強LLMs的圖形計算以進行檢索和推理；LLMs與知識圖（KGs）之間的雙向整合，其中LLMs支持KG的構建和管理，而KG則強制執行語義約束和事實一致性；以及通過圖形算法增強的AI代理，用於規劃、決策和多步推理。與此同時，LLMs通過自然語言接口和混合LLM-圖形神經網絡（GNN）管道引入了圖形數據管理和圖形機器學習（ML）的新能力。本教程綜合了推動這些融合方向的算法、系統和設計原則，為數據科學和數據挖掘研究人員提供了將LLMs、圖形數據管理、圖形挖掘、圖形ML和代理計算整合到下一代圖形原生AI系統中的統一視角。

docs.title: Privacy-Preserving Federated Autoencoder for ECG Anomaly Detection on Edge Devices
docs.text:
2606.11556v1 by Kaan Arda Akyol, Jakub Kacper Szeląg, Aydin Abadi, Maha Alghamdi, Ghadah Albalawi, Ghouse Ibrahim Kaleelullah, Hilal Tutus, Sarah Al Subaiei, Shardul Kapse, Syed Mohammed Raheeb, Mujeeb Ahmed, Rehmat Ullah

Continuous electrocardiography (ECG) monitoring could surface rhythm abnormalities before they escalate into cardiovascular events. However, a deployable system must satisfy three requirements simultaneously: legal-grade privacy (GDPR, HIPAA), real-time inference on constrained edge hardware, and detection quality under non-IID cross-hospital data. We design and evaluate an end-to-end federated system addressing all three for unsupervised 12-lead ECG anomaly detection on PTB-XL dataset, combining three autoencoder families (VanillaAE, ConvAE, VAE), Flower-based federated averaging (FedAvg) across ten simulated hospitals, client-side differentially private SGD (DP-SGD) with a Rényi-DP accountant, and 8-bit integer (INT8) post-training quantization with Raspberry Pi 4 benchmarking. Our main contributions are: an empirical characterization of how these mechanisms compose, practical DP-specific recommendations, and technical and security insights for a clinically sensitive setting. Federated learning matches or exceeds the centralized baseline across all architectures (ConvAE federated area under the ROC curve, AUROC, $0.782$), and an $\varepsilon$ sweep identifies $\varepsilon=4$ as the recommended clinical operating point. INT8 quantization roughly halves model size and cuts Pi 4 latency by up to $44%$ with $<0.12%$ AUROC loss. Crucially, DP and quantization penalties are empirically independent, so practitioners need not trade a strong privacy guarantee for a compact edge footprint. To our knowledge, this is the first system combining federated learning, formal $(\varepsilon,δ)$-DP, unsupervised reconstruction-based detection, and quantized AArch64 deployment.

摘要：持續的心電圖（ECG）監測可以在心血管事件升級之前發現心律異常。然後，一個可部署的系統必須同時滿足三個要求：法律級別的隱私（GDPR、HIPAA）、在受限邊緣硬體上的即時推斷，以及在非獨立同分佈的跨醫院數據下的檢測質量。 我們設計並評估了一個端到端的聯邦系統，針對PTB-XL數據集進行無監督的12導聯ECG異常檢測，解決了這三個問題，結合了三個自編碼器家族（VanillaAE、ConvAE、VAE）、基於Flower的聯邦平均（FedAvg）在十個模擬醫院之間進行、客戶端差異隱私隨機梯度下降（DP-SGD）與Rényi-DP會計師，以及使用Raspberry Pi 4基準測試的8位整數（INT8）後訓練量化。我們的主要貢獻是：對這些機制如何組合的實證特徵描述、實用的DP特定建議，以及對臨床敏感環境的技術和安全見解。聯邦學習在所有架構中都匹配或超過集中基準（ConvAE的ROC曲線下的聯邦面積，AUROC，$0.782$），並且一個$\varepsilon$掃描確定$\varepsilon=4$為推薦的臨床操作點。INT8量化大約將模型大小減半，並將Pi 4的延遲減少多達$44\%$，且AUROC損失小於$0.12\%$。關鍵是，DP和量化懲罰在實證上是獨立的，因此從業者不必為了緊湊的邊緣佔用而犧牲強大的隱私保證。據我們所知，這是第一個結合聯邦學習、正式的$(\varepsilon,δ)$-DP、無監督重建基礎檢測和量化AArch64部署的系統。

docs.title: SkillJuror: Measuring How Agent Skill Organization Changes Runtime Behavior
docs.text:
2606.11543v1 by Zhiyu Chen, Zihan Guo, Bo Huang, Bingwei Lu, Jianghao Lin, Yuanjian Zhou, Weinan Zhang

Agent Skills augment large language model (LLM) agents with procedural knowledge at inference time, but current benchmarks rarely distinguish what a Skill says from how it is organized. We study this distinction through Progressive Disclosure, where a concise root file points agents to supporting resources on demand, and compare it with a normalized flat baseline. We present SkillJuror, a framework for evaluating Skill writing paradigms through semantically controlled variants, matched multi-trial evaluations, and trajectory evidence while holding task knowledge fixed. In an 82-task SkillsBench study, Progressive Disclosure changes runtime behavior before aggregate outcomes: distinct Skill resources touched per trajectory rise from 1.18 to 3.85, and effective uptake events rise from 1.33 to 3.92. It also yields 17 additional verifier-passing trials out of 410 matched trials (+4.1%) over the normalized flat baseline. The benefit is task-dependent. Progressive Disclosure helps when supporting resources guide implementation, checking, or repair, but is weaker when success hinges on exact output conventions, numerical thresholds, or long artifact-generation pipelines. These results show that Skill organization is not mere presentation: it can change how agents search and apply procedural knowledge, while outcome gains depend on whether the exposed resources are actionable for the task. Code is available at https://github.com/zhiyuchen-ai/skill-juror.

摘要：代理技能在推理時增強大型語言模型（LLM）代理的程序知識，但目前的基準很少區分技能的內容與其組織方式。我們通過漸進式披露來研究這一區別，其中一個簡潔的根文件根據需求指向支持資源，並將其與標準化的平坦基準進行比較。我們提出了SkillJuror，一個通過語義控制變體、匹配的多次試驗評估和軌跡證據來評估技能寫作範式的框架，同時保持任務知識不變。在一項82任務的SkillsBench研究中，漸進式披露在總體結果之前改變了運行時行為：每條軌跡接觸的不同技能資源從1.18上升到3.85，有效的獲取事件從1.33上升到3.92。它還在410次匹配試驗中產生了17個額外的驗證者通過試驗（+4.1%），超過了標準化的平坦基準。這一好處是依賴於任務的。當支持資源指導實施、檢查或修復時，漸進式披露有助於任務，但當成功取決於精確的輸出約定、數值閾值或長期產物生成管道時，其效果較弱。這些結果表明，技能組織並非僅僅是展示：它可以改變代理搜索和應用程序知識的方式，而結果增益則取決於所暴露的資源是否對任務可行。代碼可在 https://github.com/zhiyuchen-ai/skill-juror 獲得。

docs.title: Hubs or Fringes: Pretraining Data Selection via Web Graph Centrality
docs.text:
2606.11499v1 by Vedant Badoni, Danqi Chen, Xinyi Wang

The performance of modern language models depends critically on pretraining data composition. Yet existing data selection methods rely on auxiliary classifiers for document scoring or mixture optimization, adding computational overhead and dependence on labeled data. We propose WebGraphMix, a lightweight data selection framework that computes structural centrality scores over the Common Crawl host-level web graph and uses them to vary the proportion of central versus peripheral documents in the pretraining mixture. We hypothesize that central hosts expose models to reusable abstractions, while peripheral hosts encode specialized, long-tail knowledge. WebGraphMix computes centrality scores efficiently at web scale, requiring no model training, labeled data, or downstream supervision. We integrate WebGraphMix into the DataComp-LM pipeline and train models at 400M and 1B parameter scales with 8B and 28B tokens respectively, evaluating on 23 tasks ranging from factual knowledge to symbolic reasoning. Our experiments show that central and peripheral web regions encode complementary capabilities. Mixture combining both at a ratio of 1:1 achieves 41.4% on average, compared to 39.8% for uniform sampling. Combining structural scores with document-level quality classifier scores further improves performance to 43.8%. These findings demonstrate that web graph topology is a meaningful axis for pretraining data curation, capturing information that is largely orthogonal to existing content-based approaches.

摘要：現代語言模型的性能在很大程度上依賴於預訓練數據的組成。然而，現有的數據選擇方法依賴於輔助分類器進行文檔打分或混合優化，這增加了計算開銷並依賴於標記數據。我們提出了WebGraphMix，一種輕量級的數據選擇框架，該框架計算Common Crawl主機級別網絡圖的結構中心性分數，並利用這些分數來改變預訓練混合中中心文檔與周邊文檔的比例。我們假設中心主機使模型接觸到可重用的抽象，而周邊主機則編碼專門的長尾知識。WebGraphMix在網絡規模上高效計算中心性分數，無需模型訓練、標記數據或下游監督。我們將WebGraphMix整合到DataComp-LM管道中，並在400M和1B參數規模上訓練模型，分別使用8B和28B標記，並在23個任務上進行評估，這些任務涵蓋了從事實知識到符號推理的範疇。我們的實驗顯示，中心和周邊的網絡區域編碼了互補的能力。以1:1的比例混合兩者的結果平均達到41.4%，而均勻抽樣則為39.8%。將結構分數與文檔級質量分類器分數結合進一步提高了性能，達到43.8%。這些發現表明，網絡圖拓撲是一個有意義的預訓練數據策劃軸心，捕捉到的信息在很大程度上與現有的基於內容的方法正交。

docs.title: Building Social World Models with Large Language Models
docs.text:
2606.11482v1 by Haofei Yu, Yining Zhao, Guanyu Lin, Jiaxuan You

Understanding and predicting how social beliefs evolve in response to events -- from policy changes to scientific breakthroughs -- remains a fundamental challenge in social science. Given LLMs' commonsense knowledge and social intelligence, we ask: Can LLMs model the dynamics of social beliefs following social events? In this work, we introduce the concept of the Social World Model (SWM), a general framework designed to capture how social beliefs evolve in response to major events. SWM learns state-transition functions for social beliefs by mining temporal patterns in social data and optimizing the evidence lower bound, without the need for explicit human annotations linking events to belief shifts, or for expensive census data. To evaluate SWM, we introduce a benchmark, SWM-bench, derived from real-world prediction markets, specifically Kalshi and Polymarket. SWM-bench includes over 12k data points for social belief prediction tasks spanning diverse domains such as politics, finance, and cryptocurrency. Our experimental results show that SWM significantly outperforms time-series foundation models, achieving state-of-the-art results on Kalshi data and demonstrating competitive performance on Polymarket data, while offering interpretable insights into the underlying mechanisms of social belief dynamics.

摘要：理解和預測社會信念如何隨著事件（從政策變更到科學突破）而演變，仍然是社會科學中的一個基本挑戰。考慮到LLMs的常識知識和社會智慧，我們提出：LLMs能否模擬社會事件後社會信念的動態？在這項工作中，我們引入了社會世界模型（SWM）的概念，這是一個旨在捕捉社會信念如何隨重大事件而演變的通用框架。SWM通過挖掘社會數據中的時間模式並優化證據下界，學習社會信念的狀態轉換函數，而無需明確的人類註釋將事件與信念變化聯繫起來，也無需昂貴的普查數據。為了評估SWM，我們引入了一個基準，SWM-bench，該基準源自現實世界的預測市場，特別是Kalshi和Polymarket。SWM-bench包含超過12,000個數據點，用於涵蓋政治、金融和加密貨幣等多個領域的社會信念預測任務。我們的實驗結果顯示，SWM的表現顯著優於時間序列基礎模型，在Kalshi數據上達到最先進的結果，並在Polymarket數據上展現出競爭力的表現，同時提供了對社會信念動態基本機制的可解釋見解。

docs.title: INFRAMIND: Infrastructure-Aware Multi-Agent Orchestration
docs.text:
2606.11440v1 by Ahasan Kabir, Jiaqi Xue, Mengxin Zheng, Qian Lou

Existing multi-agent LLM orchestration methods, ranging from brute-force ensembles to learned routers, select models and topologies based on task and model features. However, these methods do not consider the runtime state of the serving infrastructure. On shared GPU clusters under concurrent load, this infrastructure blindness causes systematic resource underutilization: preferred models accumulate deep request queues while equally capable alternatives sit idle. In multi-agent pipelines, where each query triggers multiple sequential model calls, these delays then compound across every downstream step. Closing this gap is challenging because the relevant infrastructure signals (queue depths, KV-cache pressure, latencies) are dynamic and noisy, and they must drive three different decisions: planning, per-step routing, and scheduling. We introduce INFRAMIND, a framework that makes the entire multi-agent stack infrastructure-aware. An infra-aware planner conditions topology and role selection on real-time system load and remaining budget, biasing toward simpler graphs under congestion and richer ones at low load. An infra-aware executor then observes per-model queue depths, cache utilization, and response latencies at each agent step to decide which model to call and how deeply to reason; a budget-aware scheduler further reorders each model's queue so that urgent requests are served first. Cast as a hierarchical constrained MDP and solved end-to-end via reinforcement learning, the system learns to balance quality against latency automatically. Across five benchmarks, INFRAMIND delivers up to +7.6 pp accuracy over the prior baseline at low load with up to 7x lower latency, and sustains up to 99.9% SLO compliance under high load where every baseline drops below 50%.

摘要：現有的多智能體 LLM 協調方法，從暴力集成到學習路由器，根據任務和模型特徵選擇模型和拓撲。然而，這些方法並未考慮服務基礎設施的運行時狀態。在共享 GPU 集群下的並發負載中，這種基礎設施盲點導致系統性資源未充分利用：首選模型積累了深度請求隊列，而同樣能夠的替代方案則閒置。在多智能體管道中，每個查詢觸發多個連續的模型調用，這些延遲在每一步驟中進一步累積。填補這一空白具有挑戰性，因為相關的基礎設施信號（隊列深度、KV 緩存壓力、延遲）是動態且嘈雜的，並且它們必須驅動三個不同的決策：規劃、每步路由和調度。我們介紹了 INFRAMIND，一個使整個多智能體堆棧具備基礎設施感知的框架。一個具備基礎設施感知的規劃器根據實時系統負載和剩餘預算對拓撲和角色選擇進行條件設定，在擁擠時偏向於較簡單的圖形，而在低負載時則偏向於較豐富的圖形。一個具備基礎設施感知的執行器則在每個智能體步驟中觀察每個模型的隊列深度、緩存利用率和響應延遲，以決定調用哪個模型以及推理的深度；一個具備預算感知的調度器進一步重新排序每個模型的隊列，以便優先處理緊急請求。該系統被視為一個分層約束 MDP，並通過強化學習端到端求解，自動學習在質量與延遲之間取得平衡。在五個基準測試中，INFRAMIND 在低負載下提供了比先前基準高出 +7.6 pp 的準確度，並且延遲降低了最多 7 倍，在高負載下保持了高達 99.9% 的服務水平協議合規性，而每個基準則降至 50% 以下。

docs.title: Towards a Bridge Layer Between Bibliographic and Formalized Mathematical Knowledge
docs.text:
2606.11430v1 by A. Mayeux

Mathematical knowledge is split between bibliographic databases (e.g., MathSciNet, zbMATH Open) and formal proof libraries (e.g., Lean mathlib), preventing unified access between published results and their formalizations. We propose a relational bridge-database that aligns publication metadata with formal artifacts, providing an interoperability layer between mathematical literature and machine-verifiable proofs. We introduce a paper-level formalization score that measures how much of a publication is covered in formal systems.
